tell: Pure decoration with no content: a gradient/blurred blob or orb Image parked behind the layout. Carries zero information.
check_ugui: In the target subtree, Image nodes that have no Text/Selectable, no children and no interaction, whose name or sprite reads as blob/glow/orb/gradient = 0.
check_uitk: USS has no gradient property. Look for decoration-only VisualElements (no children, no text), typically taken out of the flow with position: absolute, drawn with a generated background-image or with filter where that property exists (6000.3+ schema buckets).
exception: A single focal instance kept as brand identity is not a tell. The tell is reflexive repetition and screen-wide decoration unrelated to content.
fix: Delete the decorative node and sweep the orphaned sprite/material.

tell: A uniform Shadow/Outline component dropped onto most Graphics for no stated reason. Reads as noise, not emphasis.
check_ugui: Count Graphics carrying a Shadow or Outline with identical parameters (exec: GetComponentsInChildren<Shadow>()). 3 or more repeats with the same settings = violation.
check_uitk: UI Toolkit has no element shadow (no box-shadow in USS), so element-level shadow is a uGUI concern only. For text, the same repetition in text-shadow or -unity-text-outline-width across 3+ elements = violation.
exception: One deliberate emphasis surface is fine.
fix: Remove the uniform Shadow/Outline and carry emphasis with figure-ground instead: value contrast, size, and whitespace.

tell: Decorative vertex gradient switched on for TextMeshPro text.
check_ugui: manage_components get --type TMP_Text: m_enableVertexGradient == true on more than one text = violation.
check_uitk: UI Toolkit text takes a single color; there is no text gradient in USS, so this tell does not apply (uGUI only).
exception: One hero/identity instance is fine.
fix: Set m_enableVertexGradient to false and use a solid color (manage_components set).

tell: Emoji used as icons, bullets, or navigation affordances.
check_ugui: Text/TMP_Text acting as an icon, button label, or nav item whose m_text contains emoji codepoints (U+1F300 and up, U+2600 and up) = violation.
check_uitk: Label/Button text containing emoji codepoints = violation.
exception: Emoji that are part of the content copy are untouchable.
fix: Replace with a real sprite icon or a text label; preserve the meaning of the copy.

tell: Status-shaped dots that track no status, AI-branding sparkles, and chart imagery with no data behind it.
check_ugui: Sprite nodes shaped like status dots, sparkles, or charts that no script or state drives = 0. Treat a node as driven only when a component on it or its parent reads/writes that state.
check_uitk: Same, over decoration-only VisualElements with no bound data source.
exception: Dots that do reflect real state (online, unread, cooldown) are not a tell.
fix: Delete the decorative node.

tell: Frosted-glass surfaces: a translucent fill stacked on a blur.
check_ugui: Image with alpha below 1 backed by a blur material = violation; the surface should be opaque and solid.
check_uitk: filter: blur() plus a translucent background-color. USS filter exists only in the 6000.3+ schema buckets; below that a blur cannot be expressed in USS at all, so this tell does not apply there.
exception: One deliberate overlay is fine.
fix: Replace with an opaque solid surface.

tell: A full-screen mesh or aurora gradient laid in as a decorative backdrop.
check_ugui: Full-screen gradient/mesh Image layers carrying no information = 0.
check_uitk: USS has no gradient property, so this appears as a full-screen generated background-image used purely as decoration = 0.
exception: One backdrop that is genuinely part of the brand identity is fine.
fix: Remove the decorative layer and let the background go flat.

tell: Glow and bloom baked into the art itself, sprinkled across many elements at once.
check_ugui: Emissive or bloom-baked sprites and glow materials repeated on 3 or more elements = violation. Repeated Shadow/Outline components belong to shadow-outline-overuse, not here — the two must not both fire on one defect.
check_uitk: Glow drawn into the background-image asset, repeated across 3 or more elements = violation. For text, repeated bright text-shadow is shadow-outline-overuse's concern.
exception: One focal glow is fine.
fix: Strip the repeated glow and keep at most one focal instance.

tell: Decorative emoji scattered through sentences and labels.
check_ugui: TMP_Text whose m_text carries emoji beyond the content's own meaning, across several texts = violation.
check_uitk: Same, over Label text.
exception: Emoji the content itself calls for are untouchable.
fix: Remove the decorative emoji and leave the wording intact.

tell: A UI Graphic renders in shader-error magenta because its material is broken or unresolved.
check_ugui: Graphics whose material is non-null but whose shader does not resolve (exec: inspect material.shader.name for the error shader) = 0.
check_uitk: UI Toolkit renders through USS and has no shader-error color, so this tell does not apply.
fix: Reassign the material or fall back to the default UI material.

tell: Generated art with the usual signature: flat corporate-vector people, plastic 3D renders, over-smoothed stock imagery, generic mark-like logos, filler avatar rows.
check_ugui: Not a source-measurable predicate. Tools cannot see pixels; an agent with vision must read the sprite/texture itself. Hera supplies only the sprite path, size and palette metadata.
check_uitk: Same: the background-image asset has to be judged visually.
exception: Deliberate art direction is not a tell.
fix: Mark candidates for replacement; the actual art call belongs to a human or an art pass, not to this tool.

tell: The anchoring and scaling strategy does not match what the screen is for.
check_ugui: A screen built to fill the display whose root anchors are fixed rather than stretched, or whose CanvasScaler is ConstantPixelSize, needs review; a dense data screen is the mirror case.
check_uitk: Root flex-grow / percentage sizing versus fixed px, read together with PanelSettings.scaleMode, should match the screen's purpose.
exception: Genuinely dense data screens (inventory, tables, dashboards) are meant to be fixed and contained.
fix: Settle on one strategy for the screen and apply it. Filling the viewport means aligning to it, not stretching the content inside.

tell: A surface (Image background, border, or shadow) wraps a child that carries the same kind of surface, so two surfaces say the same thing.
check_ugui: Parent-Image-surface directly wrapping child-Image-surface pairs, counting only those where the child has fewer than 3 same-shaped siblings AND no interaction component AND holds a single short element = 0.
check_uitk: Same, over .uxml nesting where both parent and child carry background-color or border USS, filtered by the same gate.
exception: Functional surfaces are excluded: (1) same-size cells repeated 3 or more times under a LayoutGroup/Grid, such as inventory slots and hotbars, (2) surfaces carrying Button/Toggle/EventTrigger or a drop-target script, (3) panels that hold an independent, dense unit of information. Judged without this gate, every cell of an inventory grid reads as a false positive; nested surfaces are usually functional in game UI.
fix: Flatten only decorative nesting: strip the inner surface, replace it with a divider and spacing, and keep at most one surface per group.

tell: An empty RectTransform wrapper that contributes nothing to layout.
check_ugui: Nodes with exactly one child, RectTransform as their only component, and anchors that add no layout behaviour = 0 (exec: GetComponents().Length == 1 && childCount == 1).
check_uitk: VisualElements with one child and no inline style, USS class, or layout contribution = 0.
exception: Responsive breakpoint containers, scroll/mask hosts, and semantic landmarks do contribute and are not ghosts.
fix: Unwrap the child onto the parent and delete the empty node.

tell: A thin colored strip pinned along one edge, usually the left, used as emphasis.
check_ugui: Colored Images 8 px or thinner in one dimension, aligned to a parent edge = 0.
check_uitk: A thick colored border on a single side (border-left-width with border-left-color, asymmetric to the other sides) = 0.
fix: Remove the strip and carry emphasis with figure-ground: value contrast, size, and whitespace.

tell: Plain rows and one-line items each wrapped in their own surface, so the surface separates nothing.
check_ugui: Items in a repeated series that each carry an Image surface while holding only a short string or one or two fields AND carrying no interaction component = violation.
check_uitk: Repeated items each carrying background-color/border/border-radius USS while holding the same thin content and taking no input = violation.
exception: Runs the same functional-surface gate as box-in-box, and the two must agree — an item is exempt when it carries interaction (Button/Toggle/EventTrigger or a drop-target script) or holds a dense unit of its own (title, body, metadata, an action). Inventory slots and hotbar cells keep their surface even though their content is thin, because the surface is the target you click and drop onto.
fix: Strip the surface and separate with a hairline divider and spacing instead.

tell: Column and container widths come from nowhere: fixed px here, content-fit there, so adjacent sections do not line up.
check_ugui: Widths of sibling and adjacent content containers, and LayoutGroup tracks, should derive from one measure token or a harmonic fraction of it. Content-fit rows with arbitrary widths = violation.
check_uitk: width / flex-basis USS values should derive from one scale. USS has no grid and no fr unit, so equal cells are flex-grow: 1 with flex-basis: 0.
exception: A single prose column is meant to be narrow: cap it around 45-75 characters per line.
fix: Derive width from one measure token and force equal cells with flex-grow rather than letting content decide.

borrow.rule: Judge by density (column count, element count), never by screen size alone.

tell: Bullets, numbers, and icons in a repeated series are centred vertically, so on any item that wraps to two lines the marker drifts off the first line.
check_ugui: Series marker RectTransforms whose pivot/anchor sits at the vertical centre (0.5) rather than the top = violation.
check_uitk: A marker row using align-items: center while items can wrap = violation. Unity's Align enum offers flex-start, flex-end, center, stretch and auto (there is no baseline value), so pin markers with flex-start.
exception: Irrelevant when every item is guaranteed to be one line.
fix: Pin the marker to the first line, and right-align numbering to a fixed digit width.

tell: A sprite rendered larger than its source, so it renders soft.
check_ugui: Image render size (RectTransform rect) must stay within the sprite's native rect; anything larger = violation.
check_uitk: background-size larger than the source = violation; check -unity-background-scale-mode alongside it.
exception: For 9-sliced sprites, judge the centre region and ignore the border.
fix: Cap at native size. An odd image that does not fit a series should be dropped rather than stretched.

tell: A detailed illustration or icon rendered so small that its detail is unreadable.
check_ugui: A high-resolution sprite rendered far below its native size (roughly under half) loses the detail it was drawn for; review it.
check_uitk: A background-image displayed far below its source resolution; review it.
exception: Texture-like decoration is unaffected.
fix: Render it large enough to read, or swap in a simpler icon drawn for that size.

tell: Every surface carries the same large corner radius for no stated reason.
check_ugui: uGUI Image has no radius property; rounding lives in the sprite's pixels or a custom shader, so this cannot be read from serialized properties. Judge it from the sprite assets in use, or skip the tell on uGUI.
check_uitk: border-radius (the four border-*-*-radius properties) carrying the same large value across every surface = violation.
exception: A design system that declares one radius scale is disciplined, not slop.
fix: Derive radius from a declared scale and let informational surfaces and interactive surfaces differ.

tell: RectTransform anchors left at the bottom-left default, so the layout breaks the moment the resolution changes.
check_ugui: Elements that should stretch or centre but whose anchorMin equals anchorMax at (0,0) = violation (read anchors via manage_ui).
check_uitk: UI Toolkit has no anchors, but the same failure appears as layout built from position: absolute with hardcoded left/top offsets. Absolute elements leave the normal flow and do not reflow when the panel resizes, so flag absolute positioning used for ordinary layout rather than for a deliberate overlay; relative flow (the default) with flex adapts on its own.
exception: Fine when bottom-left pinning is actually intended, or when an element is a deliberate overlay that should sit outside the flow.
fix: On uGUI apply the right named preset with manage_ui (center, stretch, and so on) while preserving the visible rect. On UI Toolkit move ordinary layout back to relative flow with flex sizing and keep position: absolute for overlays only.

tell: Raycast Target left on for text and decoration that will never be clicked, which both swallows input and costs performance.
check_ugui: Graphics with no interaction whose m_RaycastTarget is true, on 3 or more nodes = violation; text and decorative Images normally want it off.
check_uitk: VisualElement.pickingMode (the UXML picking-mode attribute and the C# property, not a USS property) left at Position on non-interactive elements = violation; they want Ignore.
exception: Real click and drag targets keep it on.
fix: Turn m_RaycastTarget off on decoration and text, and set pickingMode to Ignore on the UI Toolkit side.

tell: The root Canvas has no CanvasScaler, or it is left on ConstantPixelSize, so the UI is pinned to pixels and breaks across resolutions.
check_ugui: Root Canvas has a CanvasScaler with uiScaleMode ScaleWithScreenSize, a referenceResolution set, and screenMatchMode Expand. Unity defaults to ConstantPixelSize at 800x600, so an untouched scaler fails this.
check_uitk: PanelSettings.scaleMode is a scaling mode rather than ConstantPixelSize, with referenceResolution set.
exception: Deliberate pixel-locking, as in pixel art, is fine.
fix: Set Scale With Screen Size, give it a reference resolution (1280x720 is a common working standard; 1920x1080 and 1600x900 are also widely used), set Screen Match Mode to Expand so the UI scales proportionally, and leave Reference Pixels Per Unit at 100. Fix this at project start — retrofitting resolution handling is expensive. Verify across 16:9, 19.5:9, 21.5:9, 4:3, 1:1 and 2:1 before calling it done.

tell: A button that looks clickable but is wired to nothing.
check_ugui: Buttons whose m_OnClick.m_PersistentCalls is empty and that no script targets = violation. Listeners added at runtime are not serialized, so this check sees only the persistent ones.
check_uitk: Buttons with no clickable handler registered = violation.
exception: Controls explicitly shown as disabled (interactable false) are a separate case.
fix: Wire the action, or present the control as disabled. The copy stays untouched.

tell: Placeholder content shipped as if it were real: Lorem text, New Text, Item 001, default sprites, invented numbers.
check_ugui: Text/TMP m_text matching placeholder patterns (Lorem, New Text, Item <n>, TODO), or Images left on a default/None sprite = violation.
check_uitk: Label text matching the same patterns, or an unset background-image = violation.
exception: Editor and tooling screens that are meant to show sample data are a separate case.
fix: Bind real data or drop in a representative sample. With no data to show, handle it as an empty state instead.

tell: A dynamic list or grid with no empty, loading, or error state, so zero data means a blank screen.
check_ugui: Whether a list/grid container has a state view or a script branch for empty, loading and error. This needs the owning script read alongside the scene, not scene state alone.
check_uitk: Whether the dynamic list has state views or USS class branches for the same three cases.
exception: Static screens whose data is always present are unaffected.
fix: Add the empty, loading and error views. This is a content decision, so propose rather than apply it silently.

tell: Almost every interaction routed through a full-screen popup, until the flow is nothing but modals.
check_ugui: Count full-screen overlay panels (a screen-covering Image with a blocker). 3 or more distinct overlays in one flow warrants review.
check_uitk: The same count over full-screen overlay VisualElements.
exception: Interactions that genuinely must block, such as confirmations and payments, are legitimate modals.
fix: Demote to inline panels, sheets, or tooltips as the information hierarchy allows. This changes the flow, so propose it.

tell: Navigation or a sidebar built from icons alone, so nothing says what the destinations are.
check_ugui: Nav or sidebar items holding an Image but no Text/TMP label; review when this holds for most items.
check_uitk: Nav items with an icon element and no Label; same review.
exception: Relaxed when the icons are universal (home, settings) and a tooltip is present.
fix: Put the text label alongside the icon, or add an expanded labelled state.

tell: UI Toolkit only: styling written as inline style attributes throughout the UXML, with no .uss file and no token layer. The code signature of a missing design system.
check_ugui: Not applicable to uGUI.
check_uitk: Many UXML elements carrying inline style attributes with no matching .uss file or class = violation; no custom properties (--name) collected on a :root rule strengthens it.
exception: Relaxed for one-off prototypes and single elements.
fix: Lift inline style into USS classes and collect tokens (--color-*, --space-*) on :root.

tell: Spacing picked by eye, so nothing establishes hierarchy: overline, title and body all sit the same distance apart and the block reads as one lump.
check_ugui: LayoutGroup spacing and padding, and the space between elements, should all be a derived base multiplied by a fixed rung. Literal values chosen ad hoc = violation.
check_uitk: USS has no gap property, so sibling spacing is expressed with margin-* (and padding-* on the container). Those values should sit on the same rung ladder.
fix: Derive the base from the screen's character, then hold the tiers: hug 0.25x, within 1x, action 2x, component 4x, section 8x. Keep hug larger than the title's own line-height step.

borrow.rule: Unity ships no spacing scale — LayoutGroup spacing and padding both default to 0 — so hera declares this one. Values are reference-resolution pixels: the ladder assumes a 1280x720 reference resolution (CanvasScaler on uGUI, PanelSettings on UI Toolkit) and scales with it, so multiply by 1.5 against a 1920x1080 reference. Base 4 px with the fixed multiples 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 24; snap derived values to the nearest rung. A design system already declared in the project always wins over this default.

tell: Nearly every gap is too tight, because generated layouts rarely leave room.
check_ugui: Gaps and padding sitting uniformly below the derived base = violation.
check_uitk: margin-* and padding-* uniformly below the derived base = violation.
exception: Functionally dense screens such as tables and inventories are meant to be tight.
fix: Assume overcrowding as the default failure and raise the base, then re-derive the ladder from unscaled-spacing-ladder.

tell: Uniform spacing everywhere, so there is no grouping and everything reads as one undifferentiated block.
check_ugui: Spacing inside a focus group equal to the spacing between groups (no tier separation) = violation.
check_uitk: The same equality across margin-* inside versus between groups = violation.
fix: Tighten inside groups to the hug/within tiers and open up between them at the component/section tiers, using the ladder from unscaled-spacing-ladder.

tell: A large empty region with no purpose, typically under a call to action or between sections.
check_ugui: An empty region with no content occupying a significant share of the view = violation, especially an even grid holding more cells than it has content for.
check_uitk: The same, including empty flex space left over from a container sized for more.
exception: On a deliberately low-density screen, generous framing whitespace is composition, not void.
fix: Delete and recompose: re-centre, re-proportion, regroup. Recomposition means moving the existing content, never adding decoration to fill the hole.

tell: The spacing inside a focus group (a hero, for example) is expanded uniformly until the group falls apart.
check_ugui: Spacing between focus elements should stay at or below the group's outer margin; wider inside than outside = violation.
check_uitk: The same comparison across margin-* inside the group versus around it.
fix: Keep the group tight and spend the whitespace on the margin around it, so the group reads as one object.

tell: Decorative italics, typically one word of a headline set apart for no reason.
check_ugui: manage_components get --type TMP_Text: m_fontStyle & Italic == 0 across every text. Legacy Text serializes the same flag at m_FontData.m_FontStyle.
check_uitk: -unity-font-style accepts normal, italic, bold and bold-and-italic, so italics are fully expressible here. The check is that every resolved -unity-font-style is normal or bold, never italic or bold-and-italic.
fix: Clear the Italic bit on the uGUI side and set -unity-font-style to normal (or bold) in USS.

tell: Typefaces mixed with no role behind them: one face wandering across roles, or one role served by several faces.
check_ugui: The mapping between TMP_FontAsset and role (display, label, body) should be one-to-one. Only a broken mapping needs fixing.
check_uitk: The same one-to-one check over -unity-font-definition.
exception: Three faces are a system, not slop, as long as each serves exactly one role. Leave a disciplined system alone.
fix: Fix the roles, not the count. Build hierarchy from size, weight and spacing rather than by swapping typefaces.

tell: Font sizes chosen ad hoc, so there is no scale underneath the hierarchy.
check_ugui: Every TMP m_fontSize should land on a rung of a base x ratio scale; a spread of unrelated literals = violation.
check_uitk: The same check over font-size in USS.
fix: Snap observed sizes to the nearest rung while preserving the existing order of emphasis.

borrow.rule: Unity ships no type scale — legacy Text defaults to 14 and TextMeshPro to 36, neither of which forms a system — so hera declares this one. Sizes are reference-resolution pixels against a 1280x720 reference resolution (CanvasScaler on uGUI, PanelSettings on UI Toolkit) and scale with it, so multiply by 1.5 against 1920x1080. Body sits at 16, stepped by a 1.2-1.25 modular ratio and rounded to whole pixels. Snap observed sizes to the nearest rung. Absolute px means nothing without the reference resolution, so state it alongside any size you report. A type scale already declared in the project wins over this default.

tell: Display type blown far past the top of the scale for no reason.
check_ugui: The largest TMP m_fontSize sitting well beyond the top rung of the type scale = violation.
check_uitk: The same over the largest font-size.
exception: A deliberately bold, low-density screen earns large type, as long as the size still comes off the scale.
fix: Cap at the top rung of the scale in unscaled-type-hierarchy.

tell: Hierarchy faked by blowing up a single word instead of using the scale.
check_ugui: An extreme size or weight jump applied to one fragment inside otherwise same-role text = violation.
check_uitk: The same, applied to one inline fragment.
fix: Build hierarchy from scale rungs and weight, and keep one-off emphasis restrained.

tell: The primary font has no Hangul glyphs, so Korean text jumps to a fallback mid-string and the shape and weight visibly shift.
check_ugui: The TMP_Text fontAsset plus its fallback chain should cover every Hangul character actually displayed; a missing glyph forcing a fallback = violation. Measure against real Korean strings.
check_uitk: The same coverage check over -unity-font-definition and its fallbacks.
exception: Irrelevant on English-only UI.
fix: Move to a font that includes Hangul, or point the fallback at a Korean face that matches the primary visually.

tell: Korean lines break mid-word because wrapping ignores word boundaries.
check_ugui: TMP wrapping settings should preserve word boundaries on sentence text; prose breaking character by character = violation.
check_uitk: white-space governs wrapping in USS (there is no word-wrap property); on sentence text it must not force character-level breaks.
exception: Fixed-width cells and numeric fields are unaffected.
fix: Keep word-boundary wrapping on prose, and widen the measure instead of forcing a narrow column.

tell: A numbered overline, an all-caps kicker, or a pill badge stuck above a one-off section purely as decoration.
check_ugui: A label or number above a title where the section has no repeating siblings, and the label either restates the title or is a bare index with nothing anchoring it = violation. The restatement half needs a semantic read, not a property read.
check_uitk: The same, over a Label acting as an eyebrow above a one-off section.
exception: Keep it only when it sits on 2 or more repeating sibling blocks and carries a category or ordinal the title does not.
fix: Delete the decorative eyebrow, number, or badge; it is not content. If kept, tie it to the title with hug spacing.

tell: Every item in a series painted a different hue, so colour codes nothing.
check_ugui: Accent Graphic colors across sibling items in one series resolving to different hues = violation.
check_uitk: The same across color/background-color on repeated items.
exception: Colour that marks a real semantic state (success, warning, error) is legitimate.
fix: Collapse to one accent plus neutrals, and add no decorative hues.

borrow.rule: One accent hue plus a neutral value ramp. Colour roles are limited to brand, the primary action, and states that actually exist (error, warning, success); everything else goes neutral.

tell: Every HUD stat, number and label carries identical visual weight, so nothing important stands out.
check_ugui: Stat and label text whose fontSize, weight and color are uniform regardless of importance = violation.
check_uitk: The same over font-size, font weight and color in USS.
exception: A comparison table meant to read as equal is unaffected.
fix: Emphasise only the numbers that matter, using size, weight and contrast from the type scale, and demote labels and secondary values.

tell: Chromatic colour sprayed across content that carries no state, so hue means nothing.
check_ugui: Any chromatic Graphic outside the declared roles (brand, primary action, real states) = violation. The role map has to be stated first; it cannot be derived from the scene.
check_uitk: The same over chromatic color/background-color outside those roles.
exception: Brand, the primary action, and error/warning/success are the only roles colour earns.
fix: Demote everything outside those roles to the neutral ramp and sweep the orphaned color variables and materials.

borrow.rule: One accent hue plus a neutral value ramp; converge everything else onto neutrals.

tell: Several highly saturated hues competing at once.
check_ugui: 3 or more distinct high-saturation hues coexisting across the screen = violation.
check_uitk: The same across USS colors.
exception: An art direction that deliberately calls for many colours is a different case, provided it is a stated decision.
fix: Converge on one or two accents plus the neutral ramp.

borrow.rule: One accent hue plus a neutral value ramp.

tell: Body text that does not clear the accessibility contrast threshold against its background.
check_ugui: For each text, compute the luminance contrast between the foreground (TMP m_fontColor, or m_Color on legacy Text/Graphic) and the background Graphic's m_Color; body text needs 4.5:1 and large or UI text 3:1. Read the values with manage_components get and compute.
check_uitk: The same computation between color and background-color in USS.
exception: Purely decorative, non-informational text may be judged at the large-text threshold.
fix: Adjust the foreground lightness first, keeping the hue, and reach for a higher step on the neutral ramp.

borrow.rule: Move the foreground rather than repainting the background.

tell: Surfaces painted in iridescent or rainbow gradients.
check_ugui: Multi-hue gradients used on surfaces or text = violation.
check_uitk: USS has no gradient property, so this appears as a multi-hue generated background-image on a surface = violation.
exception: One focal instance kept as brand identity is fine.
fix: Replace with the neutral ramp plus a single accent.

borrow.rule: Neutral value ramp plus one accent hue.
